documentation.value: ArchiMate 3.1 issue-driven redesign approved for sidepanel transcript fragmentation and semantic confusion. The fix extends existing brownfield software units SU-SP-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION, SU-SP-CONVERSATION-VIEWPORT, and SU-SP-RUN-STREAM-CONTROLLER under the mandatory human-authored TODO contracts on ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT, ELM-FUNC-EXT-RENDER-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT, and ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX. Design decision: no new software unit is introduced. The projection layer must aggregate assistant content by stable run-level conversation ownership rather than by changing SSE messageId values, must not synthesize ghost user nodes from tool/status/text transitions or out-of-order payloads, and must emit one renderer-ready assistant message node per runId. The render layer must hard-guarantee one assistant article per runId, place reasoning and tool-call parts only inside ProcessDisclosure native details markup, place only final result content inside FinalAnswerPanel, and preserve React node identity during high-frequency tail deltas so the assistant article and disclosure subtree do not remount.

designDecisions: ADR-1 Brownfield fit decision: extend existing modules extension/src/shared/api.ts, extension/src/shared/protocol.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/model.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/diagnostics.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx, and extension/src/sidepanel/components/stage/MainStage.tsx. Rejected creating a new standalone observability subsystem because the required ownership seams already exist and a new subsystem would increase coupling and UX regression risk.

ADR-2 Observability contract decision: introduce a Transcript Pipeline Observability Service and Transcript Trace Evidence data object so the same logical transcript-bearing item can be correlated across transport receipt, event generation, normalization, sidepanel acceptance, transcript projection, diagnostics export, and final render analysis.

ADR-3 UX preservation decision: diagnostics remain passive to the user experience. Instrumentation may add trace metadata, console/export evidence, and render-path analysis, but must not change normal sidepanel transcript content, transcript ordering semantics, or visible interaction behavior.

ADR: Brownfield fit for transcript anti-fragmentation fix. Selected modules are extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx, extension/src/sidepanel/components/stage/MainStage.tsx, and extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts. Rejected introducing a new transcript semantic normalizer software unit because the existing controller -> projection -> viewport seam already matches the required ownership boundaries and allows the mandatory TODO contracts to be implemented without adding coupling.

ADR: Transcript ownership model for AC11-AC13. Within a single runId, assistant DOM ownership is run-scoped and must remain singular even when SSE emits multiple semantic.messageId or data.message_id values. Semantic routing is strict: thinking renders only as reasoning parts, tool_call/tool_result render only as tool parts inside the process disclosure tree, and result/final answer content renders only inside FinalAnswerPanel. Renderer topology is stable: tail updates may mutate active assistant content but must not create, remount, or fragment assistant articles.

[Input]: Active browser tab URL, optional host permission patterns, stored page rules, and Chrome runtime permission state.
[Processing]: Check whether the page is restricted, whether the domain is within the controllable authorization list, whether an enabled rule matches the page, and whether runtime host permission has been granted before capture or run startup.
[Output]: A page-access decision containing allowed or blocked state, matched-rule context, permission guidance, or a domain error.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Restricted pages are rejected; unmatched rules block capture; unauthorized domains surface an explicit authorization path; authorized matched pages proceed consistently.

[Processing]: Optionally capture page data, derive username context, normalize captured fields, reuse or create the session context, call the adapter start-run API, and initialize run state for downstream SSE streaming.
[Output]: A started run with run id, selected agent, optional session id, and initialized extension run record.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Capture-plus-run and follow-up flows both work; startup failures surface normalized errors; successful startup persists active run and session state for the side panel.

[Input]: Partial or full assistant state updates from background actions, run lifecycle changes, and side-panel sync requests.
[Processing]: Merge state with defaults, persist it to chrome.storage.local, timestamp the update, and broadcast STATE_UPDATED so background and side-panel consumers stay aligned.
[Output]: Durable shared extension state for capture, run, error, and session context.
[Acceptance Criteria]: State writes preserve main-agent and run context; broadcasts follow persistence; refreshed UI views observe coherent status and error data.

[Input]: Requested field definitions, authorized document context, window context, DOM selectors, and optional fallback values.
[Processing]: For each enabled field rule, read the configured source such as document title, URL, selected text, meta tag, selector text, or selector attribute, normalize whitespace, and apply fallback values when extraction is empty.
[Output]: A captured field map keyed by configured field identifiers such as software_version, selected_sr, pageTitle, pageUrl, or selectedText.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Enabled configured fields are extracted deterministically; missing values fall back as configured; unauthorized or unavailable pages do not silently fabricate capture results.

[Input]: Authorized document and window objects containing DOM attributes, text nodes, meta tags, and optional global user variables.
[Processing]: Search supported DOM selectors first, then configured user-related meta tags, then page-global variables, and normalize the first reliable username evidence with its source classification.
[Output]: A username context containing username text and source such as dom_data_attribute, dom_text, meta_tag, page_global, unknown_fallback, or unresolved_login_state.
[Acceptance Criteria]: DOM-based identity evidence takes precedence over meta and globals; the result includes a provenance label; pages lacking evidence fall back to unknown rather than incorrect identities.

[Input]: Authorized page DOM readiness state and extension runtime messaging capabilities.
[Processing]: Create a floating AI button once per page, wire its click action to request panel opening, and toggle an embedded iframe panel only when side-panel APIs are unavailable and fallback is needed.
[Output]: An in-page assistant entrypoint that opens the side panel or embedded fallback UI for the current business page.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The floating button appears at most once per page; clicking it triggers extension messaging; embedded fallback panels can be opened and closed without duplicating UI containers.

[Processing]: Maintain an explicit conversation projection state machine that separates raw event flow from UI transcript grouping; create User Message Nodes only from verified user prompt or answer sources; bind assistant reasoning, tool_call, and text emissions to one stable Assistant Message Node using semantic.messageId or data.message_id with provisional run-scoped fallback; prevent assistant tool/text status transitions, empty payloads, or out-of-order SSE injections from opening synthetic User nodes; finalize assistant nodes only on terminal state or verified next-user-turn boundaries; emit structural and tail-oriented projection metadata while preserving historical node identity and anomaly diagnostics. [Output]: Stable transcript read model containing deterministic message nodes, active assistant node identity, sealed historical segments, tail-update metadata, summary state, and anomaly diagnostics suitable for renderer consumption. [Acceptance Criteria]: GIVEN a long streaming assistant answer with reasoning, tool_call, and final text phases, WHEN SSE status switches between tool and text, THEN the projection preserves one Assistant Message ID and renders no empty User bubble; out-of-order or empty SSE payloads do not create new User nodes; assistant nodes remain open until terminal state; historical transcript identity remains stable while live tail updates incrementally.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Reconcile Cross-Component Run State governs background-sidepanel snapshot reconciliation so accepted live events, persisted history, and rebroadcast state do not reintroduce duplicate, missing, or out-of-order transcript data.
[Input]: Background-stored assistant state, sidepanel synchronization payloads, active run identifiers, accepted event-frontier metadata, snapshot-origin metadata, and current browser-storage state.
[Processing]: Compare incoming run-state snapshots against the stored active-run frontier, derive a reconciliation decision per snapshot, reject stale or replayed snapshots, merge only monotonic event-frontier advances for the active run, preserve accepted events and answers already stored, and emit structured synchronization diagnostics for accepted, rejected, stale, divergent, or replayed updates.
[Output]: A reconciled canonical extension run state suitable for persistence and rebroadcast to sidepanel consumers without reintroducing duplicate, missing, or out-of-order events.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Background synchronization never overwrites a newer active-run snapshot with an older one; replayed sidepanel payloads do not duplicate accepted events after refresh or reopen; divergence between stored and incoming frontiers is logged with run id and decision outcome; rebroadcast state reflects only reconciled canonical data.

# Formal Requirement Draft

## Goal
Restore and formalize the Chrome extension sidepanel capability for authorized webpage capture so users can intentionally fetch webpage content by configured rules, include that captured content with their prompt/question in backend AI requests, and prevent this behavior from becoming implicit or disappearing again.

## Scope
This requirement covers:

1. **Sidepanel UI restoration**
   - Restore the sidepanel control/button that allows the user to fetch content from the active authorized webpage according to configured capture rules.
   - The control must be available in the intended sidepanel interaction path and must no longer be a hidden or accidental capability.

2. **Capture-to-run payload explicitness**
   - Make the product behavior explicit that webpage-captured data can be combined with the user’s prompt/question and then sent to the backend AI run-start flow.
   - Preserve the brownfield behavior evidenced by:
     - capture entry via `RECAPTURE` / `collectFromActiveTab`
     - orchestration path via `startRunFromActiveTab(...)`
     - backend request packaging including `prompt`, `selectedAgent`, `capture`, and `context`
     - backend adapter acceptance of `capture` and `context` in `RunStartRequest`

3. **Architecture knowledge graph explicitness**
   - Add or update architecture knowledge so this capability is documented as an intentional, traceable system behavior rather than an implicit coupling across UI, background orchestration, shared API, and backend adapter layers.

4. **Regression-proof testing**
   - Add regression tests that protect:
     - the sidepanel capture-entry UI/control presence and expected interaction
     - the flow from captured webpage data into the backend AI request payload

## Backward Compatibility & Resolved Conflicts
- **Historical context found in graph**: No relevant prior architecture requirements, constraints, or business processes were found in the knowledge graph search.
- **Conflict analysis**: No graph-documented conflict was detected.
- **Brownfield compatibility expectation**: This requirement is intended to **restore and formalize** already-evidenced brownfield behavior rather than introduce a new product concept. Existing capture-capable code paths and payload fields must remain supported and become explicitly protected by architecture documentation and tests.

## User Stories / Usage Scenarios
1. **As an extension user**, I want a visible sidepanel control to fetch content from the current authorized webpage, so I can intentionally include webpage data in my AI-assisted workflow.
2. **As an extension user**, I want captured webpage content to be sent together with my prompt/question when starting an AI run, so the backend AI can use both my instruction and the captured page evidence.
3. **As a maintainer/architect**, I want this capability explicitly represented in the architecture knowledge graph, so future changes do not accidentally remove or obscure it.
4. **As a maintainer**, I want regression tests covering both UI entry and backend payload composition, so this behavior does not silently disappear again.

## Acceptance Criteria (Mandatory)

### AC1 — Sidepanel capture control is restored
**Given** the user opens the extension sidepanel in a context where webpage capture is allowed by configured authorization/rules  
**When** the sidepanel is rendered  
**Then** a visible control for webpage capture is present in the sidepanel UI.

### AC2 — Sidepanel capture control triggers capture path
**Given** the capture control is visible in the sidepanel  
**When** the user activates the control  
**Then** the extension invokes the existing or equivalent authorized active-tab capture flow for collecting webpage data by configured rules.

### AC3 — Authorization/rule boundary is preserved
**Given** the active page is not authorized or does not satisfy configured capture rules  
**When** the user attempts to use the capture capability  
**Then** the extension must not behave as if valid page content was captured, and the UI/flow must respect the configured authorization boundary.

### AC4 — Captured webpage data is included with user prompt in run-start flow
**Given** webpage content has been captured for the active tab  
**And** the user submits a prompt/question to start an AI run  
**When** the run-start request is created  
**Then** the request payload includes the user prompt/question and the captured webpage data together in the supported run-start structure.

### AC5 — Payload structure remains explicit and traceable
**Given** a run is started from the active-tab-assisted flow  
**When** the request is sent to the backend run-start API  
**Then** traceable evidence exists in code and/or tests that the payload packaging includes the supported fields for `prompt`, `selectedAgent`, `capture`, and `context`, with `capture` representing webpage-captured data when present.

### AC6 — Backend compatibility is preserved
**Given** the backend adapter receives a run-start request containing captured webpage data  
**When** the request is validated/handled  
**Then** the request shape remains compatible with the backend model accepting `capture` and `context` in `RunStartRequest`.

### AC7 — Architecture knowledge graph is updated
**Given** this requirement is approved and implemented downstream  
**When** architecture documentation/knowledge graph updates are made  
**Then** the graph must explicitly describe:
- the sidepanel UI entry point for webpage capture
- the orchestration path that can capture page data before run start
- the request payload relationship between user prompt/question and captured webpage data
- the backend acceptance of capture-bearing run-start requests

### AC8 — Regression test for sidepanel UI entry exists
**Given** the repository test suite is executed after the change  
**When** UI regression tests run  
**Then** there is a test that fails if the sidepanel capture control/entry capability disappears again.

### AC9 — Regression test for capture-to-backend payload exists
**Given** the repository test suite is executed after the change  
**When** flow/integration/unit tests covering run-start payload creation execute  
**Then** there is a test that fails if captured webpage data is no longer packaged together with the user prompt/question for the backend request.

### AC10 — Evidence remains inspectable for future maintainers
**Given** a future maintainer inspects the repository and architecture artifacts  
**When** they trace this capability  
**Then** they can identify the UI entry, orchestration path, payload packaging, backend contract, and regression tests without relying on tribal knowledge.

## Constraints & Non-functional Requirements
- Do not define this as an implicit behavior only discoverable from code; it must be explicit in architecture knowledge.
- The requirement is for a **brownfield restoration/formalization**, so downstream design should preserve verified existing payload semantics unless an explicit architectural change is approved later.
- Regression coverage must be durable enough to detect both:
  - UI removal/regression
  - payload composition regression
- The capability must remain bounded by configured authorization/capture rules for webpage access.
- The requirement must support end-to-end traceability across UI, orchestration, API packaging, backend contract, and tests.

## Open Questions / Assumptions
- Assumption: “authorized webpage by configured rules” refers to the repository’s existing authorization/capture-rule mechanism and does not require new policy design in this requirement.
- Assumption: The exact button label may vary by product language/UI copy, but the user-visible capture entry itself is mandatory.
- Assumption: The architecture graph update may be implemented through one or more linked architecture elements, as long as the capability becomes explicit and traceable.

An ApplicationService represents externally visible automated behavior offered by application components to support business behavior. In this architecture, Authorized Webpage Capture Run-Start Service makes explicit the end-to-end capability in which the sidepanel exposes a visible capture entry, the extension captures authorized active-page data according to configured rules, and the run-start flow preserves that captured data together with the user prompt/question in the backend request contract.

A DataObject represents passive application data that is created, read, updated, or transported by application behavior. In this architecture, Capture-Enriched Run-Start Payload is the explicit request data structure used for backend run start when a user prompt/question is combined with selectedAgent, optional capture derived from the authorized active webpage, and context metadata such as capturedAt, username, usernameSource, pageTitle, and pageUrl.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Expose Sidepanel Capture Entry governs the visible operator-facing sidepanel control that preserves webpage capture as an intentional UI action and dispatches recapture into the extension flow.
[Input]: User interaction with the sidepanel composer area, current assistant busy state, and the visible capture-control rendering state.
[Processing]: Render and preserve an explicit sidepanel capture control, prevent invocation while the assistant is busy, and dispatch the recapture command through the controller so capture remains an intentional operator action rather than an implicit side effect.
[Output]: A visible capture entry in the sidepanel UI and a dispatched recapture request when the user activates it.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The rendered sidepanel includes a visible capture control in the intended interaction path; activating the control dispatches the recapture flow; regression tests fail if the control disappears or no longer dispatches recapture; the UI does not bypass existing authorization boundaries.

name.value: Orchestrate Authorized Capture And Run Start

documentation.value:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Orchestrate Authorized Capture And Run Start governs the background-side orchestration that enforces authorization and rules, collects webpage data when requested, and preserves capture-aware run-start handoff semantics.
[Input]: User prompt/question, selected main agent, optional session id, capture-page intent, active-tab metadata, stored rules, and runtime authorization state.
[Processing]: Enforce active-tab authorization and configured rule boundaries, optionally collect page fields from the authorized active tab, derive username context, preserve matched-rule state, and pass the resulting prompt plus captured webpage data into the shared run-start packaging flow without changing the supported payload semantics.
[Output]: An authorized capture result and a run-start handoff containing prompt, selected agent, optional captured webpage data, and context inputs for request packaging.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unauthorized or unmatched pages do not produce a false successful capture; authorized pages can still capture and start a run; regression tests fail if captured webpage data stops flowing from background orchestration into run-start handoff; existing payload semantics remain compatible with prompt, selectedAgent, capture, and context.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Package Capture-Enriched Run-Start Request governs the shared API behavior that builds the backend /api/runs request so captured webpage data remains explicitly packaged together with the user prompt/question when present.
[Input]: User prompt/question, selected main agent, optional captured webpage data, username context, and optional session id.
[Processing]: Normalize optional capture content, build the run-start HTTP request body, preserve the supported fields prompt and selectedAgent, include capture when present, and always include context metadata compatible with the backend contract.
[Output]: A POST /api/runs request payload containing prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture, optional sessionId, and context.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Regression tests fail if captured webpage data is no longer packaged together with the user prompt/question when present; send-only flows still omit capture correctly; payload structure remains inspectable and compatible with the existing run-start contract; tests verify prompt, selectedAgent, capture, and context semantics.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Accept Capture-Bearing RunStartRequest governs backend request-model acceptance so capture-bearing run-start payloads remain compatible with the adapter's existing request validation and startup flow.
[Input]: Backend POST /api/runs request containing prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture payload, optional sessionId, and context metadata.
[Processing]: Validate the incoming RunStartRequest model, preserve backend compatibility for capture and context fields, and hand the accepted request to adapter run-start processing without dropping capture-bearing payload support.
[Output]: A validated backend run-start request accepted for adapter processing or a normalized validation failure.
[Acceptance Criteria]: RunStartRequest continues to accept capture and context; regression tests fail if backend acceptance removes either field or breaks capture-bearing /api/runs requests; accepted requests remain compatible with the existing adapter start-run flow.

An ApplicationService represents externally visible automated behavior offered by application components. In this architecture, Stable Transcript Projection Service exposes the sidepanel contract that converts accepted live and historical run data into a stable transcript read model with deterministic message identity, terminal-state semantics, anomaly reporting, and render-friendly patch metadata for the UI layer.

An ApplicationService represents externally visible automated behavior offered by application components. In this architecture, Incremental Conversation Renderer Service exposes the sidepanel UI contract that consumes a stable transcript read model, preserves sealed message identity, and applies local tail updates for active assistant output without whole-list rerender or whole-transcript markdown reparsing.

[Input]: Stable transcript read model, active assistant node identity, structural patch notifications, tail delta payloads, feedback UI state, auto-follow state, and transcript action metadata. [Processing]: Render transcript messages from the read-model contract instead of raw SSE events; keep sealed messages referentially stable; accumulate high-frequency assistant text deltas in refs or equivalent buffers; flush tail updates through debounce, requestAnimationFrame, or memoized local patch application; update only the active assistant message tail and defer markdown reparsing to the affected active block; preserve precise DOM bubble counts and action affordances when assistant phases alternate between reasoning, tool, and text. [Output]: Incrementally updated transcript DOM with stable historical message subtrees, locally updated active assistant tail, accurate final markdown, and scroll behavior that follows live updates without whole-list rerender churn. [Acceptance Criteria]: GIVEN SSE emits single-character tokens at very high frequency such as every 10ms, WHEN React renders the stream, THEN ref buffering plus debounce or memoization keeps the UI smooth and preserves exact markdown output without omissions; each token does not trigger whole transcript rerender or full markdown reparse; DOM never shows more than the intended user and assistant bubbles for a single turn under tool/text alternation.
TODO:[CRITICAL RENDERER FIX - REQUIRED IMMEDIATELY]
1. **[DOM Anti-Fragmentation]**: 视图层必须硬性保证：一个 `runId` 在 DOM 中只能且必须只渲染出一个 `<article class="transcript-message-assistant">`。严禁出现幽灵 User 节点。
2. **[Visual Layering]**: 在该唯一的 `<article>` 内部，必须严格执行物理分层：
   - `ProcessDisclosure` 组件：负责统一渲染所有的 `reasoningParts` 和 `toolParts`，必须使用原生 `<details>` 标签包裹并默认折叠。
   - `FinalAnswerPanel` 组件：只负责渲染 `finalAnswerPart`。
3. **[Remount Prevention]**: 当 `FinalAnswerPanel` 正在高频接收增量文本（Tail Delta）时，其父级 `<article>` 及其兄弟组件 `ProcessDisclosure` 绝对不允许发生 unmount/remount（必须保持 React 节点身份稳定），以彻底消除界面的闪烁和卡顿。

name.value: Sidepanel 会话体验重构：达到 OpenCode Web 级别的丝滑流式交互体验

documentation.value:
# Requirement: ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX ## 名称Sidepanel 会话体验重构：达到 OpenCode Web 级别的丝滑流式交互体验## Goal 对当前浏览器插件 Sidepanel 的会话界面进行一次深度 UI/UX 架构重构，目标不是局部视觉修补，而是建立可持续演进的高性能会话渲染架构，使其在主观体验、交互流畅度、流式输出节奏、滚动行为与信息分层上对标 OpenCode Web 的顶级会话体验。## 核心架构性要求### R1. 组件级读写隔离- 历史消息必须封装为独立纯渲染组件。- 已完成消息必须通过稳定 props 与 React.memo 或等价冻结策略避免重复渲染。- 当前仍在流式输出的消息必须作为独立的 Active Tail 渲染单元存在。- Active Tail 的更新不得触发历史消息列表父级的整体重新渲染。- 会话状态管理必须体现“稳定历史区”与“动态流式区”的读写隔离。### R2. Markdown 增量与异步渲染优化- 禁止在每次 SSE delta 到达时对整段 Markdown 执行全量同步解析。- 流式阶段必须采用 requestAnimationFrame、节流器或等价机制控制渲染频率，上限不得高于 30fps。 - 在 terminal state 到达时，必须执行最终强制 flush，确保最终展示内容完整一致。 ### R3. 智能滚动控制- 必须实现 pin-to-bottom 行为。- 用户手动向上滚动脱离底部后，系统必须暂停自动滚动，并显示“⬇ 最新消息”悬浮提示。- 用户点击该提示后，视图应恢复到底部并重新进入自动跟随态。### R4. 思考过程与结果分离渲染- Reasoning、Tool Call、Final Answer 必须在视觉上明确区分。- Reasoning / Tool Call 必须支持折叠/展开。 - Final Answer 必须默认处于最易阅读的主展示层。## Acceptance Criteria - 历史消息在流式期间不重渲染，仅 Active Tail 更新。- 流式渲染必须经由 rAF/节流调度，实际 UI 提交频率不得高于 30fps。 - terminal state 到达时必须立即最终 flush，最终展示内容完整一致。 - 用户上滚时不得被强制拉回，且必须显示“⬇ 最新消息”提示。- Reasoning / Tool Call / Final Answer 必须清晰分层，且思考过程支持折叠/展开。
TODO:
### AC11 — 严禁 RunID 内部发生气泡裂变 (Anti-Fragmentation)
**Given** 同一个 runId 的 SSE 流中包含了多个不同的 messageId (因 Agent 切换或多步推理产生)  
**When** 投影器解析并由 React 渲染后  
**Then** DOM 树中针对该 runId 必须精确且只渲染出 1 个 Assistant 气泡节点，绝不允许被切碎成多个 article。

### AC12 — 严格的语义分层断言
**Given** 接收到 10 段 `thinking` 文本、5 个 `tool_call` 和 1 个 `result` 文本  
**When** 投影并渲染后  
**Then** 断言：`FinalAnswerPanel` 内只能包含 `result` 的内容；`thinking` 和 `tool_call` 必须被渲染在 `ProcessDisclosure` 的 `<details>` 树内部，且不在主视图暴露。

### AC13 — 工具调用过程对用户可见 (Tool Visibility)
**Given** AI 触发了工具调用 (如 'read' 或 'search')  
**When** 渲染 UI 时  
**Then** 工具的调用过程必须像 OpenCode Web 一样在折叠面板中清晰可见，严禁将其作为 diagnostic-only 数据隐藏。

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Call Adapter API governs extension-side HTTP and SSE integration contracts and exposes the raw transport metadata needed to diagnose duplicate, missing, or reordered run events.
[Input]: Prompt payloads, capture payloads, username context, selected agent, optional session id, feedback requests, optional API key configuration, and SSE connection context.
[Processing]: Build validated HTTP request bodies and headers for run start, answer submission, feedback submission, and SSE stream setup; parse normalized success or failure responses with schema validation; preserve raw event transport identifiers and semantic metadata provided by the adapter; surface connection-open, message, error, and reconnect transitions; and emit transport-level diagnostics needed to distinguish upstream duplication from extension-side replay.
[Output]: Typed extension-side API results, stream connections aligned to the adapter contract, and transport diagnostics for SSE lifecycle and payload identity.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Requests include authentication when configured; invalid or failed responses are normalized; stream payload parsing preserves available semantic identity fields; connection and reconnect transitions are observable in diagnostics; transport diagnostics allow the team to determine whether duplication originates before or after sidepanel acceptance.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Consume Normalized Run Stream governs sidepanel receipt of normalized run events, canonical acceptance of those events, persistence coordination, and observability for duplicate, missing, and out-of-order stream behavior.
[Input]: Run id, adapter SSE endpoint, normalized run events, current side-panel state, prior accepted event identities, accepted ordering frontier, and current persistence/synchronization status.
[Processing]: Open the EventSource stream, validate each incoming payload, derive a canonical event key using semantic identity and stable message metadata before falling back to runId plus sequence or raw id, accept only the first event per canonical key, reject stale or replayed events that do not advance the accepted frontier, log accepted, duplicate, gap, and out-of-order decisions, persist only accepted events, update run lifecycle state from accepted evidence, and synchronize only reconciled accepted state to background.
[Output]: An updated live conversation state containing accepted ordered run events, accepted-frontier metadata, derived run status, final output snapshots, stream connection state, and structured stream diagnostics.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Semantically duplicate events are not appended twice even when raw ids differ; accepted events advance in deterministic order or are classified as out-of-order with diagnostics; missing-sequence and replay conditions are logged with run id and canonical key; terminal evidence updates run status only from accepted events; refresh or reconnect does not cause already accepted events to be re-appended.

[Input]: Raw OpenCode global SSE events, run session context, buffered part deltas, and canonical remote-agent evidence.
[Processing]: Filter events by session, enforce primary-agent consistency, transform OpenCode status, text, reasoning, tool, question, idle, and error events into NormalizedRunEvent records, and buffer part deltas until part type is known.
[Output]: A browser-consumable stream of normalized thinking, tool_call, question, result, or error events.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Session-unrelated events are ignored; assistant text and reasoning are distinguished semantically; question and tool events are normalized for UI use; agent mismatches produce explicit runtime failure.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Persist Run History governs durable storage of canonical run artifacts so refresh, reopen, and replay use accepted event identities rather than reintroducing duplicate or unordered history.
[Input]: Run records, accepted normalized run events, answer records, session identifiers, canonical event identities, and persistence diagnostics context.
[Processing]: Persist run artifacts with stable canonical event identity metadata, upsert events idempotently for each run, preserve accepted event order and answer order, expose run detail reload APIs that return deterministic ordered artifacts, and log persistence writes, duplicate-write suppression, and ordering anomalies.
[Output]: A durable browser-side run history with per-run detail, canonical event identity metadata, answer history, and session navigation views.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Refresh or reopen replays the same accepted event set without adding duplicates; persisted events remain associated with the correct run and ordered deterministically; duplicate writes are suppressed or surfaced with diagnostics; session grouping remains stable when session ids are present.

name.value: Formal Requirement - Sidepanel SSE Transcript Pipeline Observability

documentation.value:
# Formal Requirement Draft

## Goal
Enable end-to-end observability for the sidepanel SSE/transcript pipeline so engineers can diagnose why transcript content is missing, duplicated, replayed, or rendered out of order, without changing the current sidepanel user experience or transcript semantics.

## Scope
This requirement covers diagnostic instrumentation across the existing sidepanel transcript flow, specifically the points identified in the change request evidence:

- `ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT` incremental refresh path
- `extension/src/shared/api.ts:createRunEventStream`
- `extension/src/shared/protocol.ts`
- `extension/src/shared/api.ts`
- `extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts`
- `extension/src/sidepanel/components/stage/MainStage.tsx`
- `extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx`

The requirement is limited to observability and diagnostic traceability for the existing flow from SSE ingestion through event generation, normalization/projection, timeline state handling, and UI rendering.

## Backward Compatibility & Resolved Conflicts
- **Historical graph context**: No relevant prior Requirement, Constraint, or BusinessProcess items were returned by the architecture search for this area.
- **Conflict analysis**: No explicit historical conflict was detected.
- **Backward compatibility rule**: This requirement must preserve the current sidepanel UX contract. It must not intentionally alter:
  - what transcript content is shown to end users,
  - the ordering rules already considered correct by the current product behavior,
  - stage transitions or rendering behavior except where diagnostic visibility is added,
  - network protocol behavior beyond diagnostic metadata/logging.
- **Resolution of ambiguity**: The TODO/mission is interpreted as an internal observability enhancement, not as a UX redesign or transcript behavior change.

## User Stories / Usage Scenarios
1. **As an engineer debugging transcript issues**, I want timestamped logs at each major pipeline step so I can trace where a missing or duplicate transcript segment was introduced.
2. **As an engineer investigating replay/out-of-order behavior**, I want correlation across SSE receipt, event generation, timeline ingestion, and final render so I can determine whether the defect originates upstream, in normalization, in state projection, or in rendering.
3. **As a maintainer**, I want the diagnostics to preserve current product behavior so I can safely enable analysis without introducing new user-facing regressions.
4. **As an architecture/design owner**, I want a strict cross-file observability contract so future debugging of sidepanel transcript anomalies is consistent and end-to-end.

## Acceptance Criteria (Mandatory)
1. **End-to-end diagnostic coverage**
   - Given a transcript-bearing SSE/event flow enters the extension,
   - When that flow passes through the identified pipeline components,
   - Then each required component shall emit diagnostic logs sufficient to reconstruct the sequence from receipt to render.

2. **Incremental refresh visibility**
   - Given incremental transcript refresh occurs in `ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT`,
   - When transcript content is refreshed,
   - Then diagnostics shall include time and content-oriented logging sufficient to identify what content was refreshed and when.

3. **Event stream creation visibility**
   - Given `createRunEventStream` processes incoming events,
   - When an event is received, transformed, or forwarded,
   - Then diagnostics shall record time and content-oriented details sufficient to identify the event’s progression through the stream.

4. **Event generation visibility**
   - Given `protocol.ts` and `api.ts` generate or normalize events,
   - When an event object is created, mapped, or materially changed,
   - Then diagnostics shall record time and content-oriented details sufficient to compare generated output against upstream input.

5. **Timeline ingestion visibility**
   - Given `reasoningTimeline.ts` ingests or updates timeline data,
   - When an event affects timeline state,
   - Then diagnostics shall record time and content-oriented details sufficient to determine whether duplication, omission, replay, or ordering anomalies were introduced at this stage.

6. **Render-stage visibility**
   - Given `MainStage.tsx` and `reasoningTimelineView.tsx` render transcript/timeline state,
   - When content is selected, deduplicated, replayed, or displayed,
   - Then diagnostics shall be sufficient for end-to-end analysis of missing or duplicate display behavior.

7. **Preserve UX contract**
   - Given observability instrumentation is added,
   - When the sidepanel is used normally,
   - Then the user-visible transcript behavior shall remain functionally unchanged except for defect fixes explicitly approved in a later requirement.

8. **Support anomaly diagnosis**
   - Given a reported transcript anomaly of missing, duplicate, replayed, or out-of-order content,
   - When engineers inspect the produced diagnostics,
   - Then they shall be able to determine the earliest pipeline stage at which the anomaly became observable.

9. **Consistent traceability**
   - Given logs are produced across multiple files/stages,
   - When engineers compare them,
   - Then the logs shall provide enough consistent context to correlate the same logical content/event across stages.

10. **No requirement expansion**
   - Given this mission is observability-focused,
   - When implementation/design is produced,
   - Then it shall not be interpreted as authorization to redesign transcript UX, modify business semantics, or broaden scope beyond diagnostics and traceability.

## Constraints & Non-functional Requirements
- Must preserve existing sidepanel UX and transcript contract.
- Must enable diagnosis of:
  - missing render,
  - duplicate render,
  - replayed render,
  - out-of-order render.
- Must cover ingestion, transformation, projection, and rendering stages.
- Diagnostics must include both **time** and **content-oriented** evidence.
- Requirement is architecture-affecting and cross-cutting across multiple software units/files.
- No unresolved runtime issue currently blocks this work; resolved issues `ISSUE-005` and `ISSUE-006` do not change this requirement.

## Open Questions / Assumptions
- Assumption: “time and content” means diagnostics must let engineers compare when a unit of transcript/event data was observed and what meaningful payload/content it represented.
- Assumption: This requirement does not mandate a specific logging backend, format, or retention policy; those are architecture/design decisions.
- Assumption: Any performance/privacy guardrails for logging will be defined in the architecture/design phase if needed.

**Proposed strict requirement statement**

The system shall add end-to-end observability across the existing sidepanel SSE/transcript pipeline so that engineers can trace transcript-bearing data from event receipt through event generation, normalization, timeline state updates, and final rendering, using diagnostics that capture time and content-oriented evidence at each required stage. This observability shall allow reliable diagnosis of missing, duplicate, replayed, or out-of-order transcript rendering while preserving the current sidepanel UX contract and transcript semantics.

An ApplicationService represents externally visible automated behavior offered by application components. In this architecture, Transcript Pipeline Observability Service exposes the cross-stage diagnostic contract that correlates transcript-bearing evidence from SSE receipt through normalization, sidepanel acceptance, transcript projection, diagnostics export, and final render analysis without changing transcript UX semantics.

A DataObject represents passive application data made available for automated behavior. In this architecture, Transcript Trace Evidence stores correlated time-oriented and content-oriented diagnostic records that let engineers compare the same logical transcript-bearing item across transport, generation, normalization, acceptance, projection, export, and render stages.

An ApplicationComponent represents modular, deployable application structure that encapsulates automated behavior. In this architecture, Shared Run Stream Contract Tracer extends the existing shared API and protocol seam in extension/src/shared/api.ts and extension/src/shared/protocol.ts to capture transport, canonicalization, and normalized event generation evidence for transcript-bearing SSE traffic.

An ApplicationComponent represents modular, deployable application structure that encapsulates automated behavior. In this architecture, Sidepanel Run Stream Controller extends the existing sidepanel controller and model seam in extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts and extension/src/sidepanel/model.ts to trace event acceptance, frontier movement, lifecycle reconciliation, and synchronization evidence.

An ApplicationComponent represents modular, deployable application structure that encapsulates automated behavior. In this architecture, Sidepanel Transcript Projection Engine extends the existing reasoning timeline seam in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts to trace incremental transcript projection, anomaly detection, stable message identity, and visibility decisions while preserving renderer-facing semantics.

An ApplicationComponent represents modular, deployable application structure that encapsulates automated behavior. In this architecture, Sidepanel Transcript Diagnostics Exporter extends the existing diagnostics export seam in extension/src/sidepanel/diagnostics.ts to assemble a correlated engineer-facing snapshot of transport, acceptance, projection, and render evidence for one run.

An ApplicationComponent represents modular, deployable application structure that encapsulates automated behavior. In this architecture, Sidepanel Conversation Viewport extends the existing render seam in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx and extension/src/sidepanel/components/stage/MainStage.tsx to analyze final transcript consumption, visible ordering, tail revision use, and render-path selection without altering the displayed UX contract.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Capture Transport and Canonical Trace governs shared-layer receipt, parsing, canonicalization, and normalized event generation evidence for transcript-bearing SSE traffic.
[Input]: Raw SSE message events, parsed stream payloads, runId, raw event ids, event sequence values, semantic identity fields, transcript-bearing content fields, and normalized shared API/protocol event objects.
[Processing]: Capture receipt timestamps, parse outcomes, canonical identity derivation source, normalization outputs, and content-oriented evidence for transcript-bearing events while preserving existing normalized event semantics and API contracts.
[Output]: Correlated transport-stage and generation-stage trace records plus normalized run events that carry stable downstream correlation metadata.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Transport diagnostics cover createRunEventStream receipt and parsing; shared protocol and API event generation and normalization are traceable; correlation fields remain stable for the same logical event across later stages; parse or normalization failures are distinguishable from successful event generation; no user-visible transcript behavior changes are introduced.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Trace Stream Acceptance Frontier governs sidepanel event-ingestion observability for acceptance decisions, frontier advancement, persistence coordination, and synchronization evidence.
[Input]: Normalized run events from the shared stream contract, current accepted event list, current RunEventState frontier metadata, lifecycle state, persistence state, and background synchronization metadata.
[Processing]: Correlate each incoming event with acceptance decisions, frontier-before and frontier-after values, duplicate or replay classification, persistence actions, and background sync transitions without changing acceptance semantics.
[Output]: Acceptance-stage trace records and updated run-event diagnostics that show whether each event was received, accepted, rejected, persisted, and synchronized.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Engineers can distinguish received-but-rejected from accepted events; duplicate, stale replay, gap, and out-of-order decisions are explicitly traceable; frontier movement is recorded before and after each decision; accepted-event behavior remains functionally unchanged for the sidepanel UX.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Trace Incremental Transcript Projection governs transcript-read-model observability for event-to-message mapping, incremental refresh behavior, and anomaly classification.
[Input]: Accepted normalized events, historical transcript segments, live transcript segment, answer records, previous projection state, stable message identifiers, and projection anomaly signals.
[Processing]: Trace event-to-message and part mapping, active-versus-sealed transitions, tail patch revision changes, hidden-versus-visible transcript decisions, and anomaly detection outcomes during ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT incremental refresh while preserving transcript semantics.
[Output]: Projection-stage trace records, enriched projection debug evidence, and correlated anomaly details suitable for diagnostics export and render analysis.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Engineers can determine whether loss, duplication, replay, or reordering first appears during projection; incremental transcript refresh is explicitly covered; active tail and sealed history behavior remain stable; transcript IDs and user-visible semantics are preserved.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Analyze Final Transcript Render Consumption governs final viewport observability for transcript render-path selection, visible ordering, and projected-versus-rendered comparison.
[Input]: TranscriptReadModel data, fallback transcript parts, presentation state, run status, stream status, render content revision values, tail patch revision values, and disclosure state.
[Processing]: Record which render path is used, which transcript parts or messages are selected for visible rendering, their final order, and any discrepancy between projected transcript state and rendered transcript state without modifying the user-visible transcript contract.
[Output]: Render-stage analysis records correlated with upstream transport, acceptance, and projection evidence.
[Acceptance Criteria]: MainStage and reasoningTimelineView render analysis paths are explicitly covered; engineers can distinguish projected-but-not-rendered cases from not-projected cases; render diagnostics remain non-visual and do not change sidepanel behavior; final visible ordering is reconstructable from the evidence.

An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Assemble Correlated Transcript Diagnostics Snapshot governs the sidepanel diagnostics export behavior that merges cross-stage observability evidence into one engineer-consumable run snapshot.
[Input]: Run metadata, transport trace records, acceptance diagnostics, projection trace records, render analysis evidence, visible transcript summaries, and diagnostic-only transcript summaries.
[Processing]: Merge cross-stage evidence into a single ordered diagnostics snapshot keyed by stable event and message correlation fields so engineers can compare the same logical transcript-bearing item from receipt through rendering.
[Output]: Downloadable diagnostics snapshot and log output that identify the earliest stage where a transcript anomaly became observable.
[Acceptance Criteria]: A single export contains enough evidence to trace one logical transcript-bearing item from receipt to render; visible transcript state is clearly separated from diagnostic-only evidence; the current diagnostics download flow remains intact; the export introduces no new user-facing workflow requirement.

documentation.value: ArchiMate 3.1 issue-driven redesign approved for sidepanel transcript fragmentation and semantic confusion. The fix extends existing brownfield software units SU-SP-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION, SU-SP-CONVERSATION-VIEWPORT, and SU-SP-RUN-STREAM-CONTROLLER under the mandatory human-authored TODO contracts on ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT, ELM-FUNC-EXT-RENDER-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT, and ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX. Design decision: no new software unit is introduced. The projection layer must aggregate assistant content by stable run-level conversation ownership rather than by changing SSE messageId values, must not synthesize ghost user nodes from tool/status/text transitions or out-of-order payloads, and must emit one renderer-ready assistant message node per runId. The render layer must hard-guarantee one assistant article per runId, place reasoning and tool-call parts only inside ProcessDisclosure native details markup, place only final result content inside FinalAnswerPanel, and preserve React node identity during high-frequency tail deltas so the assistant article and disclosure subtree do not remount.

name.value: Accepted stream events trigger history persistence

A Realization relationship indicates that one element implements or makes concrete another more abstract element. In this architecture, Transcript Pipeline Observability Service realizes the formal requirement for end-to-end transcript pipeline observability.

A Realization relationship indicates that one element implements or makes concrete another more abstract element. In this architecture, Shared Run Stream Contract Tracer realizes the transport and normalization portion of Transcript Pipeline Observability Service.

name.value: Run stream controller realizes consume stream

documentation.value: A Realization relationship indicates that one element implements or makes concrete another more abstract element. In this architecture, Sidepanel Run Stream Controller realizes the sidepanel run-stream consumption intent and carries the acceptance observability behavior.

A Realization relationship indicates that one element implements or makes concrete another more abstract element. In this architecture, Sidepanel Transcript Projection Engine realizes the transcript projection intent including incremental refresh observability.

A Realization relationship indicates that one element implements or makes concrete another more abstract element. In this architecture, Sidepanel Transcript Diagnostics Exporter realizes the engineer-facing export portion of Transcript Pipeline Observability Service.

A Realization relationship indicates that one element implements or makes concrete another more abstract element. In this architecture, Sidepanel Conversation Viewport realizes the final transcript rendering service and carries render-stage analysis instrumentation.

An Access relationship indicates that behavior uses or updates passive structure. In this architecture, Transcript Pipeline Observability Service creates and reads Transcript Trace Evidence to support end-to-end diagnosis.

name.value: Shared tracer assigned to capture trace

documentation.value: An Assignment relationship indicates that an active structure element performs behavioral responsibility. In this architecture, Shared Run Stream Contract Tracer is assigned to the Capture Transport and Canonical Trace function.

An Assignment relationship indicates that an active structure element performs behavioral responsibility. In this architecture, Sidepanel Run Stream Controller is assigned to the Trace Stream Acceptance Frontier function.

name.value: Projection engine assigned to projection trace

name.value: Diagnostics exporter assigned to assemble diagnostics

documentation.value: An Assignment relationship indicates that an active structure element performs behavioral responsibility. In this architecture, Sidepanel Transcript Diagnostics Exporter is assigned to the Assemble Correlated Transcript Diagnostics Snapshot function.

An Assignment relationship indicates that an active structure element performs behavioral responsibility. In this architecture, Sidepanel Conversation Viewport is assigned to the Analyze Final Transcript Render Consumption function.

An Access relationship indicates that behavior uses or updates passive structure. In this architecture, Capture Transport and Canonical Trace writes Transcript Trace Evidence for downstream diagnosis.

An Access relationship indicates that behavior uses or updates passive structure. In this architecture, Trace Stream Acceptance Frontier updates Transcript Trace Evidence with sidepanel acceptance-stage decisions.

An Access relationship indicates that behavior uses or updates passive structure. In this architecture, Trace Incremental Transcript Projection updates Transcript Trace Evidence with projection-stage mapping and anomaly information.

An Access relationship indicates that behavior uses or updates passive structure. In this architecture, Assemble Correlated Transcript Diagnostics Snapshot reads Transcript Trace Evidence to produce the engineer-facing export.

An Access relationship indicates that behavior uses or updates passive structure. In this architecture, Analyze Final Transcript Render Consumption updates Transcript Trace Evidence with final render-path and visible-ordering analysis.
